login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Configure auth with a Personal Access Token. Use --token/-t to provide the token, or run interactively. Use --force/-F to clear and re-authenticate.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Remove all stored MindMeister API credentials.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Check if API token is configured and valid.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Run `mindmeister auth refresh --help` for exact arguments and options.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `mindmeister auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Run `mindmeister auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Run `mindmeister auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `mindmeister auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Run `mindmeister auth profiles set-default --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `mindmeister auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `mindmeister auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

auth.usage_instructions: Personal Access Token authentication for MindMeister API. Get tokens from https://www.mindmeister.com/api.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of maps to return

list.usage_instructions: List all accessible mind maps. Supports --filter/-f, --properties/-p, --limit/-l (default 100), and --table/-t.

get.help: Get a map with full structure including all ideas/nodes.
get.usage_instructions: Get full map details including the complete node tree. MAP_ID is a positional argument.

create.help: Create a new mind map. Create an empty map with --title, or create a structured map with nodes and notes using --json-file. JSON file format - a list containing the root node: [ { "title": "Root Topic", "note": "Description of the root node", "children": [ { "title": "Branch 1", "note": "What this branch covers", "children": [ {"title": "Leaf Node", "note": "Leaf description"} ] }, {"title": "Branch 2", "note": "Another branch"} ] } ]
create.usage_instructions: Create a new map. Use --title/-T for a simple empty map, or --json-file/-j to create a structured map with nodes and notes from a JSON file.

update.usage_instructions: Update map title and/or description. MAP_ID is positional. Use --title/-T and --description/-d.

duplicate.usage_instructions: Create a copy of an existing map. MAP_ID is positional.

delete.usage_instructions: Delete a map by ID. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

export.help: Get export URLs for a map in various formats.
export.usage_instructions: Get download URLs for exporting a map in various formats. MAP_ID is positional.

maps.usage_instructions: CRUD operations on mind maps. List, get (with full node tree), create, update metadata, duplicate, delete, and export maps.

list.help: List all ideas/nodes in a map.

arguments.name: map_id
arguments.help: The map ID to list ideas from

list.usage_instructions: List all nodes in a map. MAP_ID is positional. Use --tree to display as a tree structure, or --table/-t for flat table.

toggle-closed.help: Toggle or set the collapsed state of a branch.
toggle-closed.usage_instructions: Toggle or explicitly set collapsed/expanded state of a node. MAP_ID and IDEA_ID are positional. Use --closed to collapse or --open to expand.

create-map.help: Create a new map with ideas/nodes. This uses the import workaround since the API doesn't support adding ideas directly.

arguments.name: title
arguments.help: Title for the root node and map

options.name: --child
options.help: Add first-level child nodes (can be repeated)

create-map.usage_instructions: Create a new map with nodes. TITLE is positional (root node title). Use --child/-c to add first-level children (repeatable). Use --json/-j for hierarchical structure.

import.help: Import a mind map from a file. Supported formats: .mm (FreeMind), .mmap, .xmind

arguments.name: file_path
arguments.help: Path to FreeMind (.mm) or other mind map file

import.usage_instructions: Import from FreeMind (.mm), .mmap, or .xmind files. FILE_PATH is positional.

create-annotated.help: Create a mind map with notes/comments on each node from a JSON file. The JSON file should contain a list with a root node object. Each node can have: - title: Node display text (required) - note: Note/comment for the node (optional) - children: List of child nodes (optional) Example JSON structure: [ { "title": "Root Topic", "note": "Description of the root", "children": [ { "title": "Branch 1", "note": "What this branch covers", "children": [ {"title": "Leaf", "note": "Leaf node description"} ] } ] } ]

arguments.name: file_path
arguments.help: Path to JSON file with annotated node structure

create-annotated.usage_instructions: Create a map from a JSON file with nodes that include titles and notes. FILE_PATH is positional. JSON format: list with root node object containing title, note, and children fields.

ideas.usage_instructions: Manage nodes within mind maps. List nodes (as table or tree), toggle collapsed state, create maps with structured nodes, and import from files.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `mindmeister cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `mindmeister cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

usage_instructions: Use for managing MindMeister mind maps -- create, list, update, export maps and manage ideas/nodes within them. Commands follow `mindmeister <group> <action>` pattern. Requires Personal Access Token via `mindmeister auth login`.
